description: Implement the POST /api/v1/auth/refresh endpoint in identity-service. This covers the RefreshTokenRequestDto with @Valid enforcement, RefreshTokenResponseDto matching the API reference shape, a JwtUtil class that generates and validates HS256-signed access tokens, a refreshToken method on AuthService that validates the persisted token record against the refresh_tokens table and issues a new access token, a custom InvalidRefreshTokenException for 401 responses, and GlobalExceptionHandler coverage for the 400 and 401 error cases defined in the API reference. Depends on the identity data layer for the RefreshToken entity and RefreshTokenRepository. Aligns with Sprint 3: Spring Security and JWT foundations.
userType: backend developer owning the Identity Service
functionality: validate a persisted refresh token record and issue a new short-lived JWT access token via POST /api/v1/auth/refresh
benefit: so clients can silently renew an expired access token without re-submitting credentials, keeping sessions alive across the JWT expiry window

stories.description: Implement the request and response DTOs for the refresh token endpoint in identity-service under com.loanflow.identity.dto.auth. RefreshTokenRequestDto carries a single @NotBlank-annotated refreshToken field so the controller can apply @Valid. RefreshTokenResponseDto matches the POST /api/v1/auth/refresh response shape from the API reference: accessToken, tokenType, and expiresIn — no nested user object.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: RefreshTokenRequestDto fails validation when refreshToken is blank
acceptanceCriteria.given: a RefreshTokenRequestDto is built with a blank refreshToken value
acceptanceCriteria.then: a constraint violation is raised identifying the refreshToken field

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: A fully valid RefreshTokenRequestDto passes all constraints
acceptanceCriteria.given: a RefreshTokenRequestDto is built with a non-blank refreshToken value

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: RefreshTokenResponseDto serialises to the documented POST /api/v1/auth/refresh shape
acceptanceCriteria.given: a RefreshTokenResponseDto is built with accessToken, tokenType 'Bearer', and expiresIn 3600
acceptanceCriteria.then: the JSON contains accessToken, tokenType, and expiresIn and does not include a nested user object

stories.summary: Add JWT utility and configuration to identity-service
stories.description: Introduce the JJWT library and a JwtUtil class to identity-service. JwtUtil reads a plaintext secret and an expiration value from application properties and exposes three methods: generateAccessToken, isTokenValid, and extractUsername. JwtProperties binds the configuration values from application.yaml under the app.jwt prefix so the values are injectable and independently testable.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: generateAccessToken produces a parseable HS256 JWT
acceptanceCriteria.given: JwtUtil is constructed with a valid secret and expiration
acceptanceCriteria.when: generateAccessToken is called with a username and role
acceptanceCriteria.then: the returned string is a three-part JWT signed with HS256 containing the correct subject and role claims

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: isTokenValid returns false for an expired token
acceptanceCriteria.given: a JWT was generated with a past expiration
acceptanceCriteria.when: isTokenValid is called with that token string
acceptanceCriteria.then: false is returned without throwing an exception

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: extractUsername returns the subject from a valid token
acceptanceCriteria.given: a JWT was generated for username 'jsmith'
acceptanceCriteria.when: extractUsername is called with that token string

tasks.summary: Add JJWT dependencies to identity-service pom.xml
tasks.description:
Add the following three dependencies to the identity-service pom.xml under <dependencies>:

<dependency>
    <groupId>io.jsonwebtoken</groupId>
    <artifactId>jjwt-api</artifactId>
    <version>0.12.6</version>
</dependency>
<dependency>
    <groupId>io.jsonwebtoken</groupId>
    <artifactId>jjwt-impl</artifactId>
    <version>0.12.6</version>
    <scope>runtime</scope>
</dependency>
<dependency>
    <groupId>io.jsonwebtoken</groupId>
    <artifactId>jjwt-jackson</artifactId>
    <version>0.12.6</version>
    <scope>runtime</scope>
</dependency>

tasks.summary: Add JWT configuration properties to application.yaml
tasks.description:
Append the following block to application.yaml:

app:
  jwt:
    secret: ${JWT_SECRET:this-is-a-dev-only-secret-key-min-256-bits-xx}
    expiration-ms: 3600000

The secret is read from the JWT_SECRET environment variable at runtime and falls back to a deterministic dev placeholder so the service starts without any environment configuration locally. expiration-ms maps to a one-hour token lifetime, matching expiresIn: 3600 in the API reference response.

stories.summary: Implement refreshToken service method on AuthService
stories.description: Add a refreshToken method to AuthService and implement it in AuthServiceImpl. The implementation looks up the stored RefreshToken record by token value, rejects the request if the record is absent, revoked, or past its expiry timestamp, then delegates to JwtUtil to generate a new access token. A dedicated InvalidRefreshTokenException drives the 401 error path in GlobalExceptionHandler.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: refreshToken returns a new access token for a valid, unexpired, unrevoked record
acceptanceCriteria.given: a RefreshToken record exists in the database that is not revoked and has not expired
acceptanceCriteria.when: AuthService.refreshToken is called with the matching token value
acceptanceCriteria.then: a RefreshTokenResponseDto is returned containing a non-empty accessToken, tokenType 'Bearer', and expiresIn 3600

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: refreshToken throws InvalidRefreshTokenException for an unknown token value
acceptanceCriteria.given: no RefreshToken record exists for the submitted value
acceptanceCriteria.then: InvalidRefreshTokenException is thrown with message 'Refresh token is invalid or expired'

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: refreshToken throws InvalidRefreshTokenException for a revoked token
acceptanceCriteria.given: a RefreshToken record exists with revoked set to true
acceptanceCriteria.when: AuthService.refreshToken is called with that token value
acceptanceCriteria.then: InvalidRefreshTokenException is thrown with message 'Refresh token is invalid or expired'

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: refreshToken throws InvalidRefreshTokenException for an expired token
acceptanceCriteria.given: a RefreshToken record exists with expires_at in the past
acceptanceCriteria.when: AuthService.refreshToken is called with that token value
acceptanceCriteria.then: InvalidRefreshTokenException is thrown with message 'Refresh token is invalid or expired'

tasks.description:
Extend the existing AuthService interface with:

RefreshTokenResponseDto refreshToken(RefreshTokenRequestDto request);

stories.summary: Add POST /api/v1/auth/refresh to AuthController and extend GlobalExceptionHandler
stories.description: Wire the refreshToken service method into AuthController as POST /api/v1/auth/refresh with @Valid body binding. Extend GlobalExceptionHandler to map InvalidRefreshTokenException to a 401 ProblemDetail and HttpMessageNotReadableException to a 400 ProblemDetail, covering all error cases in the API reference for this endpoint. Confirm the full flow with a dev-profile smoke test.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: POST /api/v1/auth/refresh returns 200 with a new access token for a valid refresh token
acceptanceCriteria.given: a valid, unexpired, unrevoked RefreshToken record exists in the database
acceptanceCriteria.when: POST /api/v1/auth/refresh is called with that token value
acceptanceCriteria.then: a 200 OK response is returned with a body containing accessToken, tokenType 'Bearer', and expiresIn 3600

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: POST /api/v1/auth/refresh returns 400 when refreshToken field is blank
acceptanceCriteria.given: a request body with a blank refreshToken is submitted
acceptanceCriteria.then: a 400 ProblemDetail response is returned with a detail identifying the refreshToken field

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: POST /api/v1/auth/refresh returns 401 for an invalid or expired refresh token
acceptanceCriteria.given: the submitted token value is not found, revoked, or expired
acceptanceCriteria.then: a 401 ProblemDetail response is returned with message 'Refresh token is invalid or expired' and path '/api/v1/auth/refresh'

tasks.summary: Add InvalidRefreshTokenException and HttpMessageNotReadableException handlers to GlobalExceptionHandler
Smoke test the refresh endpoint via dev profile
tasks.description:
Start identity-service with the dev profile and confirm:

1. Spring context loads without errors and GET /actuator/health returns 200.
2. POST /api/v1/auth/refresh with a blank refreshToken returns 400 with a ProblemDetail body identifying the refreshToken field.
3. POST /api/v1/auth/refresh with a token value that does not exist in refresh_tokens returns 401 with message 'Refresh token is invalid or expired'.
4. POST /api/v1/auth/refresh with a valid, unexpired, unrevoked token record returns 200 with accessToken, tokenType 'Bearer', and expiresIn 3600.
